title: Incident Response & Reporting for Agencies
description: Test your understanding of Ohio cyber incident reporting requirements, early response actions, and scenario-based decisions for public employees.

questions.prompt: Under Ohio law, how soon must a political subdivision report a discovered cybersecurity incident to OCIC?

choices.text: Within 24 hours
Within 7 days
Within 14 days
Within 30 days

questions.explanation: Ohio requires reporting to the Ohio Cyber Integration Center within 7 days after discovery.

questions.prompt: Under Ohio law, how soon must the Auditor of State be notified after a cybersecurity incident is discovered?

choices.text: Within 7 days
Within 10 business days
Within 30 days
Only after recovery is complete

questions.explanation: Ohio requires reporting to the Auditor of State within 30 days after discovery.

questions.prompt: Scenario: You receive multiple unexpected MFA prompts and then notice a login alert from an unfamiliar location. Nothing has crashed yet. What is the best next step?

choices.text: Ignore it because no outage has occurred
Report it immediately using your agency's incident reporting process
Wait until the end of the week to see if it happens again
Post a warning in a public forum

questions.explanation: Suspicious login activity and MFA prompts may indicate account compromise and should be escalated quickly.

questions.prompt: Which of the following should an Ohio political subdivision's cybersecurity program include?

choices.text: Identification of critical functions and risks
Incident response procedures and recovery planning
Cybersecurity training for employees based on job duties
All of the above

questions.explanation: Ohio guidance says a cybersecurity program should address critical functions and risks, detection, incident response, recovery, and employee training.

questions.prompt: Scenario: A coworker says files on a shared drive are suddenly inaccessible and a ransom note appears on screen. What should happen first?

choices.text: Delete the note and keep working
Report the incident immediately and follow agency instructions, including isolating affected systems if directed
Pay the ransom immediately to restore service
Reinstall the operating system right away

questions.explanation: Suspected ransomware should be reported immediately, and affected systems may need to be isolated according to agency guidance.

questions.prompt: Which implementation deadline is correct under Ohio guidance?

choices.text: Counties and cities by January 1, 2026; all other political subdivisions by July 1, 2026
All political subdivisions by January 1, 2027
Only counties must comply, and only by July 1, 2026
There are no implementation deadlines

questions.explanation: Ohio guidance gives January 1, 2026 for counties and cities, and July 1, 2026 for other political subdivisions.

questions.prompt: Scenario: You accidentally clicked a suspicious attachment, but nothing obvious happened. What should you do?

choices.text: Say nothing unless your computer locks up later
Report the event immediately because early warning signs matter
Delete the email and assume the risk is gone
Use your personal email to ask a friend for advice

questions.explanation: A suspicious click can still lead to compromise even if nothing obvious happens right away.

questions.prompt: True or False: Records and reports related to a cybersecurity program or reported cybersecurity incident under ORC 9.64 are public records that can always be shared freely.

choices.text: True
False

questions.explanation: ORC 9.64 exempts certain cybersecurity program and incident records from public records requirements.

questions.prompt: Scenario: A supervisor tells you not to report a likely cyber incident yet because they do not want attention before a public meeting. What is the best response?

choices.text: Agree and wait until after the meeting
Quietly delete related messages to reduce concern
Follow the agency's reporting process anyway because delaying may worsen harm and affect compliance
Post online so the public knows first

questions.explanation: Delaying reporting can increase damage and interfere with legal and operational obligations.

choices.text: An Ohio-only public records portal
The FBI's central hub for reporting cyber-enabled crime
A local government training vendor
A password recovery tool

questions.explanation: IC3 is the FBI-run Internet Crime Complaint Center for reporting cyber-enabled crime.

questions.prompt: Scenario: Your agency experiences ransomware. Leadership is discussing whether to pay. Which statement is most accurate under Ohio guidance?

choices.text: Ohio fully bans ransom payments in all circumstances
Ohio prefers a no-ransom approach, and if payment is made the legislative authority must adopt a public resolution explaining why it is in the public interest
Any employee may approve payment if systems are down
Payment decisions are never documented

questions.explanation: Ohio's Auditor of State favors a no-ransom policy, but the law allows payment only with a public resolution explaining why it is in the public interest.

questions.prompt: Scenario: A mayor's assistant asks you to email screenshots of a suspected breach to their personal account so they can review it from home. What is the best response?

choices.text: Send them immediately because leadership requested them
Only share incident information through approved channels and report the request if it seems improper
Post them to a shared public folder
Text them to multiple coworkers instead

questions.explanation: Incident details should be handled through authorized channels, not personal accounts or ad hoc sharing.

questions.prompt: Which action is usually a bad idea during a suspected incident unless specifically directed by your response team?

choices.text: Reporting quickly
Preserving suspicious emails or evidence
Wiping devices or trying to erase traces on your own
Following agency guidance

questions.explanation: Self-directed wiping or deletion can destroy evidence and interfere with response and investigation.

questions.prompt: Scenario: Your county office is hit by ransomware and leadership directs outside reporting. Which additional federal contact is specifically recommended by the FBI for ransomware victims?

choices.text: IC3 and the local FBI field office
Only the local Chamber of Commerce
Only a social media post
No federal reporting is recommended

questions.explanation: The FBI recommends contacting the local FBI field office and reporting ransomware through IC3.

questions.prompt: Scenario: You discover that a coworker transferred sensitive files to an unknown external address after receiving a fake request. The sender claims it was a misunderstanding. What should you do?

choices.text: Treat it as resolved if the sender apologizes
Report it immediately as a potential incident involving unauthorized disclosure
Delete the sent email and move on
Wait to see if anyone complains

questions.explanation: Potential unauthorized disclosure or compromise should be escalated immediately.

questions.prompt: Scenario: Your agency is located in Cincinnati-area coverage and leadership wants FBI contact information as part of the response checklist. Which is the correct local FBI field office number listed in the guidance we discussed?
